top_signals.title: Refiner Profits Are Soaring in Sign of Lingering Hormuz Snarls
top_signals.summary: US crude refiners are enjoying some of the best profit margins in years, the latest sign that even as energy shipments pick up through the Strait of Hormuz, there are lingering supply-chain kinks still rippling through global markets.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Trump Financial Disclosure Shows 21,000 Trades in 2025
top_signals.summary: President Donald Trump made more than 21,000 securities trades in his first year back in office, often in intense bursts tied to market events he created.

top_signals.title: Pull an all-nighter for the 1AM England World Cup game at these London pubs
top_signals.summary: After a nail-biting game last night Londoners are preparing to watch this weekend’s England World Cup fixture, although the kick off is at the rather unconventional time of 1AM on Monday morning. If you’re staying up on Sunday night into Monday morning to watch the game, there are plenty of options for places to book. [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Tesla Delivers a Blowout Quarter, Crushing Wall Street Estimates
top_signals.summary: Tesla delivered a much stronger-than-expected second quarter, reporting 480,126 vehicle deliveries, well ahead of Wall Street forecasts of roughly 406,600. The company also produced 451,758 vehicles during the period, signaling a meaningful improvement after a slow start to the year, according to CNBC. Shares were volatile on the news and have settled near unchanged into the cash open… The delivery total marked a sharp increase from 358,023 vehicles in the first quarter and was also significantly higher than the roughly 384,000 deliveries…
